tasks.subject: [dev-version-display-upgrade-ux] [P1] Add isDevMode to config route response and ServerConfigSchema
tasks.description:
Add the `isDevMode` boolean to the config API response and update the shared schema.

## Server Route Change

In `apps/server/src/routes/config.ts`, import `IS_DEV_BUILD` and add it to the response:

```typescript
import { SERVER_VERSION, IS_DEV_BUILD } from '../lib/version.js';

router.get('/', async (_req, res) => {
  // ... existing logic ...
  const latestVersion = await getLatestVersion(); // Returns null in dev mode now

  res.json({
    version: SERVER_VERSION,
    latestVersion,
    isDevMode: IS_DEV_BUILD,
    port: env.DORKOS_PORT,
    // ... rest unchanged ...
  });
});
```

The key change is adding `isDevMode: IS_DEV_BUILD` to the response object, right after `latestVersion`.

## Shared Schema Change

In `packages/shared/src/schemas.ts`, add `isDevMode` to the `ServerConfigSchema` object (around line 498-556):

```typescript
export const ServerConfigSchema = z
  .object({
    version: z.string().openapi({ description: 'Current server version' }),
    latestVersion: z
      .string()
      .nullable()
      .openapi({ description: 'Latest available version from npm, or null if dev mode or unknown' }),
    isDevMode: z
      .boolean()
      .openapi({ description: 'Whether the server is running a development build' }),
    port: z.number().int(),
    uptime: z.number(),
    // ... rest unchanged ...
  })
  .openapi('ServerConfig');
```

Note: Update the `latestVersion` description from `'or null if unknown'` to `'or null if dev mode or unknown'` to document the new behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `GET /api/config` response includes `isDevMode: true` when running dev build
- [ ] `GET /api/config` response includes `isDevMode: false` when running production build
- [ ] `latestVersion` is `null` in dev mode (from update-checker guard)
- [ ] `ServerConfigSchema` includes `isDevMode` boolean field
- [ ] `ServerConfig` TypeScript type includes `isDevMode` property
- [ ] The `latestVersion` OpenAPI description mentions dev mode
tasks.activeForm: Adding isDevMode to config route response and ServerConfigSchema

tasks.subject: [dev-version-display-upgrade-ux] [P2] Update Settings ServerTab for dev mode display
tasks.description:
Update the Settings Server tab to show a dev-mode indicator card instead of the version number and update notice when running a dev build. ## Implementation In `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/ServerTab.tsx`, wrap the existing version display in a conditional. Replace the version/update section (currently lines 27-45): ```typescript {/* Update notice — shown when latestVersion is newer */} {config.latestVersion && isNewer(config.latestVersion, config.version) && (
// ... update notice card ...
)} ``` With: ```typescript {config.isDevMode ? (
Development Build

Running from source — version checks disabled

) : ( <> {config.latestVersion && isNewer(config.latestVersion, config.version) && (
Update available: v{config.latestVersion}

Run{' '} npm update -g dorkos {' '} to update

)} )} ``` This import was already handled in task 2.1 — `isNewer` is imported from `@/layers/features/status`. Note: The `config` object's type `ServerConfig` (from `@dorkos/shared/types`) already includes `isDevMode` after the schema change in task 1.3. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Settings Server tab shows "Development Build" card when `config.isDevMode` is true - [ ] Shows "Running from source — version checks disabled" subtitle in dev mode - [ ] Does NOT show Version ConfigRow or update notice in dev mode - [ ] Production mode behavior unchanged (shows version + update notice if available) - [ ] Card uses consistent amber styling matching existing update notice
tasks.activeForm: Updating Settings ServerTab for dev mode display

tasks.subject: [dev-version-display-upgrade-ux] [P3] Add dismissedUpgradeVersions to UserConfigSchema and config response
tasks.description:
Add the `dismissedUpgradeVersions` field to the user config schema and include it in the `GET /api/config` response.

## UserConfigSchema Change

In `packages/shared/src/config-schema.ts`, add `dismissedUpgradeVersions` to the `ui` section:

```typescript
ui: z
  .object({
    theme: z.enum(['light', 'dark', 'system']).default('system'),
    dismissedUpgradeVersions: z.array(z.string())
      .default(() => [])
      .describe('Version strings the user has dismissed upgrade notifications for'),
  })
  .default(() => ({ theme: 'system' as const, dismissedUpgradeVersions: [] })),
```

Note: The `.default()` on the parent `ui` object must also include the new field.

## ServerConfigSchema Change

In `packages/shared/src/schemas.ts`, add `dismissedUpgradeVersions` to `ServerConfigSchema`:

```typescript
export const ServerConfigSchema = z
  .object({
    version: z.string().openapi({ description: 'Current server version' }),
    latestVersion: z.string().nullable()
      .openapi({ description: 'Latest available version from npm, or null if dev mode or unknown' }),
    isDevMode: z.boolean()
      .openapi({ description: 'Whether the server is running a development build' }),
    dismissedUpgradeVersions: z.array(z.string())
      .openapi({ description: 'Versions the user has dismissed upgrade notifications for' }),
    port: z.number().int(),
    // ... rest unchanged
  })
```

## Config Route Change

In `apps/server/src/routes/config.ts`, add `dismissedUpgradeVersions` to the GET response:

```typescript
res.json({
  version: SERVER_VERSION,
  latestVersion,
  isDevMode: IS_DEV_BUILD,
  dismissedUpgradeVersions:
    (configManager.get('ui') as { dismissedUpgradeVersions?: string[] } | undefined)
      ?.dismissedUpgradeVersions ?? [],
  port: env.DORKOS_PORT,
  // ... rest unchanged
});
```

Note: `configManager.get('ui')` returns the raw config section. We need to access `dismissedUpgradeVersions` from it and provide an empty array default.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `UserConfigSchema.ui` includes `dismissedUpgradeVersions: z.array(z.string()).default(() => [])`
- [ ] `UserConfig` TypeScript type includes `dismissedUpgradeVersions` under `ui`
- [ ] `ServerConfigSchema` includes `dismissedUpgradeVersions` array field
- [ ] `ServerConfig` TypeScript type includes `dismissedUpgradeVersions`
- [ ] `GET /api/config` returns `dismissedUpgradeVersions` (empty array by default)
- [ ] Existing config PATCH still works (can write to `ui.dismissedUpgradeVersions`)
- [ ] `USER_CONFIG_DEFAULTS` at bottom of config-schema.ts still parses correctly (default array)
tasks.activeForm: Adding dismissedUpgradeVersions to UserConfigSchema and config response

tasks.subject: [dev-version-display-upgrade-ux] [P4] Update env, config, and API documentation
tasks.description:
Update all documentation to reflect the new env var name, config fields, and API response changes.

## .env.example

In `.env.example`, find and replace the existing `DORKOS_VERSION` comment block (around line 48-49):

From:
```bash
# DorkOS version — do NOT set manually; injected at build time by the CLI build script
# DORKOS_VERSION=
```

To:
```bash
# Override version for testing upgrade notifications in dev mode.
# When set, dev detection is bypassed and this value is used as the current version.
# Example: DORKOS_VERSION_OVERRIDE=0.1.0 makes the UI show upgrade available for any version > 0.1.0
# DORKOS_VERSION_OVERRIDE=
```

## contributing/configuration.md

Add `DORKOS_VERSION_OVERRIDE` to the environment variables table (if one exists). Include:
- Name: `DORKOS_VERSION_OVERRIDE`
- Default: (none)
- Description: Override reported server version for testing upgrade UX. When set, dev mode detection is bypassed and the value is used as the current version. Example: `DORKOS_VERSION_OVERRIDE=0.1.0` simulates running an old version so the upgrade notification appears.

Also remove any reference to the old `DORKOS_VERSION` env var name.

## contributing/api-reference.md

Document the new fields in the `GET /api/config` response:
- `isDevMode` (boolean): Whether the server is running a development build (from `pnpm dev` or `tsx watch`). When true, `latestVersion` will be `null` and upgrade notifications are suppressed.
- `dismissedUpgradeVersions` (string[]): List of version strings the user has dismissed upgrade notifications for. Managed via `PATCH /api/config` with `{ ui: { dismissedUpgradeVersions: [...] } }`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `.env.example` updated with new env var name and description
- [ ] `contributing/configuration.md` documents `DORKOS_VERSION_OVERRIDE`
- [ ] `contributing/api-reference.md` documents `isDevMode` and `dismissedUpgradeVersions`
- [ ] No stale references to `DORKOS_VERSION` (old name) remain in docs
tasks.activeForm: Updating env, config, and API documentation
